title: Working Hours & Branches
content: AccessBank's service network page shows 40 branches, 52 ATMs, and 1 department, updated 02.03.2026. The network covers Baku and Absheron as well as regions such as Ganja, Sumgayit, Sheki, Shamkir, Lankaran, Khachmaz, Goranboy, Gabala, Nakhchivan, Balakan, Tovuz and other areas. For exact branch working hours, addresses and service availability, users should check accessbank.az or call the hotline. The bank's official legal address is 3 Tbilisi Avenue, Yasamal district, Baku city.

title: Customer Support Hotline
content: AccessBank's customer support hotline is available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. Customers can call *8880 (free from mobile networks) or +994 12 310 88 80 for international calls. Support is available in Azerbaijani, Russian, and English. The hotline handles queries about accounts, cards, loans, transfers, and mobile banking. For urgent issues such as lost or stolen cards, customers should call immediately to block their card.

title: Debit Card Types & myCard Features
content: AccessBank card products include myCard Junior, myCard White, myCard Black and other card options shown on the official website. Debit cards are linked to current accounts and can be used for ATM withdrawals, POS payments, online purchases and contactless payments where supported. myCard Junior is positioned for children and families, while myCard White and myCard Black are designed for daily personal, family and business spending. Customers can order cards online or at branches. For exact tariffs, delivery, limits and service fees, use the current plastic card tariffs or call *8880.

title: Credit Card and Loan Card Current Terms
content: AccessBank's official loan page lists a myCard credit card / loan card offer up to 10,000 AZN with a term of 36 months, annual interest rate 24%-26%, EAPR 24%-26%, 0% commission costs and a grace period of up to 50 days. Cashback may be available through the AccessQazanc program. Eligibility generally requires an identity card, official salary and minimum age from 25 years, but the bank may request additional documents after analysis.

title: Lost, Stolen, or Blocked Card
content: If a card is lost or stolen, the customer must immediately call the 24/7 hotline at *8880 to block it. The card can also be temporarily blocked through the AccessBank mobile app under Card Settings. A replacement card is issued within 3-5 business days and costs 10 AZN. Emergency cash withdrawal may be available at branches with passport identification. If the card was used fraudulently after loss, the customer should file a dispute claim at any branch with a written statement.

title: Card Payment Failed or Declined
content: A card payment may fail due to: insufficient balance, exceeded daily limit, card not activated, merchant not supporting the card type, or a temporary technical issue. Customers should check their balance via the mobile app or ATM. If the balance is sufficient and the card is active but payment still fails, the customer should contact support at *8880. If money was deducted but the transaction shows as failed, this is likely a pending reversal that takes 3-5 business days. If it does not reverse, the customer should file a dispute at a branch.

title: Mobile App & Internet Banking
content: AccessBank's mobile app is available on iOS (App Store) and Android (Google Play). Internet banking is accessible at ibank.accessbank.az. Features include: account balance check, fund transfers, utility payments, card management, loan repayment, and transaction history. To register, customers need their card number and the phone number linked to their account. If login fails, customers should reset their password via the 'Forgot Password' option or call *8880. The app supports biometric login (fingerprint and Face ID).

title: OTP & Login Issues
content: OTP (One-Time Password) is sent via SMS to the registered phone number for login and transaction confirmation. If OTP is not received, check that the phone number on file is correct and that SMS is not blocked. OTP expires after 3 minutes. If repeatedly failing to receive OTP, call *8880 or visit a branch to update the registered phone number. Never share your OTP with anyone, including bank staff. AccessBank will never ask for your OTP by phone or message.

title: Domestic Transfers
content: Transfers between AccessBank accounts are instant and free of charge. Transfers to other Azerbaijani banks take up to 1 business day and incur a fee of 0.5% of the amount (minimum 0.50 AZN, maximum 10 AZN). Transfers can be made via mobile app, internet banking, or at a branch. Daily transfer limit via mobile app is 5,000 AZN for standard accounts and 20,000 AZN for premium accounts. Transfers are processed on business days only; weekend transfers are queued for the next business day.

title: International Transfers (SWIFT)
content: AccessBank supports international SWIFT transfers to over 100 countries. The fee is 1% of the transfer amount (minimum 5 AZN, maximum 50 AZN) plus correspondent bank fees which vary by country. Processing time is 2-5 business days. Required details: recipient full name, IBAN or account number, bank name, SWIFT/BIC code, country, and purpose of transfer. Transfers over 10,000 USD equivalent may require supporting documents for compliance. SWIFT transfers can be initiated at branches or via internet banking.

title: Failed Transfer or Missing Payment
content: If a transfer was sent but not received by the beneficiary, first check the transaction status in the mobile app or internet banking. Domestic transfers may take up to 1 business day. If the status shows 'completed' but funds not received after 2 business days, the customer should contact support at *8880 with the transaction reference number. If money was deducted but the transfer shows as failed, a reversal is processed automatically within 3-5 business days. For urgent cases, visit a branch with transaction details.

title: Cash Loan Current Terms
content: AccessBank's current cash loan terms show loan amount from 500 AZN to 50,000 AZN, term from 6 to 48 months, annual interest rate from 9.5% to 24%, EAPR from 15%, commission from 0% to 3%, and annuity repayment. Requirements include Azerbaijani citizenship, age from 21 to 65 years, at least 6 months at the last workplace, identification and employment certificate. Final terms depend on workplace, seniority, credit history and internal bank procedures.

title: Secured, Mortgage-Backed and Lombard Loans
content: AccessBank's official loan page includes secured loans backed by real estate and lombard loans backed by gold jewelry. Secured loans are shown from 10,000 AZN to 70,000 AZN, from 12 to 59 months, with annual interest starting from 18%, 1% commission and collateral in real estate owned by the client or a first-degree relative. Lombard loans are shown from 200 AZN to 100,000 AZN, from 3 to 48 months, with annual interest starting from 18%, commission 1%-5% with minimum 10 AZN, and collateral as gold jewelry.

title: Loan Application Status
content: Loan application status can be checked via the mobile app under 'My Applications', via internet banking, or by calling *8880. Statuses include: Under Review, Additional Documents Required, Approved, Rejected, and Disbursed. If additional documents are required, the customer will receive an SMS notification. Approved loans are disbursed to the customer's AccessBank account within 1 business day of signing the agreement. If a loan is rejected, the customer may reapply after 3 months.

title: Loan Repayment
content: Monthly loan repayments are automatically deducted from the customer's AccessBank account on the due date set in the loan agreement. Customers must ensure sufficient balance before the due date to avoid late fees. Late payment fee is 0.1% of the overdue amount per day. Early repayment can be made via mobile app, internet banking, or at a branch. Partial early repayment reduces either the monthly payment or the loan term, based on the customer's preference. Repayment schedule can be viewed in the mobile app.

title: Account Opening
content: Azerbaijani citizens can open an AccessBank account at any branch with their national ID (Vin-kart). Foreign nationals require a passport and residence permit. Account opening is free. A basic current account (AZN) includes a free debit card. Accounts can also be opened in USD and EUR. The initial deposit requirement is 0 AZN for current accounts. Savings accounts require a minimum deposit of 100 AZN. Account opening typically takes 15-30 minutes at a branch.

title: Deposit Products Current Overview
content: AccessBank's official deposit page lists Term Deposit, myGoal Deposit, Instant Deposit and Flexi Deposit. Term Deposit starts from 50 AZN or USD, can run from 6 to 24 months and can earn up to 10.75% in AZN or up to 3.5% in USD, with interest paid monthly or at maturity. myGoal Deposit supports increasing deposits and terms up to 36 months. Instant and Flexi deposits are designed for flexible use, top-ups, withdrawals and capitalization depending on product rules. Exact rates can change, so customers should verify current rates before opening.

title: General Fees & Charges
content: Common AccessBank fees: SMS notification service 1 AZN/month (optional), account statement at branch 2 AZN per copy, card replacement 10 AZN, cash withdrawal at other bank ATMs 1% (minimum 0.50 AZN), cash deposit at branch 0.5% for amounts over 10,000 AZN, SWIFT transfer 1% (min 5 AZN, max 50 AZN), domestic interbank transfer 0.5% (min 0.50 AZN, max 10 AZN). Internal transfers between AccessBank accounts are always free.

title: Utility & Bill Payments
content: Customers can pay utility bills (electricity, gas, water, internet, TV) through the AccessBank mobile app and internet banking under the 'Payments' section. Supported providers include Azərenerji, Azərsu, Azərqaz, Aztelekom, Bakcell, Azercell, Nar Mobile, and many others. Payments are processed instantly. There is no fee for utility payments via the app. Payment history is saved in the app for reference. Customers can set up auto-payment for recurring bills.

title: Complaints & Dispute Resolution
content: Customers can file a formal complaint through the following channels: in-person at any AccessBank branch, via email at info@accessbank.az, by calling *8880, or through the official website contact form. Complaints are reviewed within 5 business days. For disputes related to unauthorized transactions, customers must file within 60 days of the transaction date. All complaints receive a reference number for tracking. If the customer is not satisfied with the resolution, they may escalate to the Financial Market Supervisory Authority (FIMSA) of Azerbaijan.

title: About AccessBank
content: AccessBank is one of Azerbaijan's leading retail banks, founded in 2002 and headquartered in Baku. It focuses on making banking simple, fast, and accessible for every customer. AccessBank serves hundreds of thousands of customers across Azerbaijan through over 40 branches, ATMs, a mobile app, and internet banking at ibank.accessbank.az. The bank offers a full range of products: current and savings accounts, debit and credit cards, consumer loans, mortgages, international transfers, and utility payments. AccessBank is known for fast loan approvals, a top-rated mobile app, and 24/7 customer support via *8880.

title: What Can AccessBank Help You With?
content: AccessBank's AI support assistant should help with AccessBank services including cards, cash loans, credit cards, secured loans, lombard loans, deposits, myAccess internet banking, mobile app use, online queue, online loan payment, money transfers, utility payments, deposit boxes, branch and ATM locations, tariffs and complaint channels. For identity-sensitive actions, card blocking, disputes, OTP reset, phone number updates and account changes, the assistant should guide the customer to *8880, a branch or the official website instead of collecting secret credentials.

title: Debit Cards - Benefits & Highlights
content: AccessBank's debit cards are the smart way to manage your money every day. Key benefits: (1) Instant access to your funds at over 2,000 ATMs across Azerbaijan. (2) Contactless NFC payments - just tap and go at any enabled terminal. (3) Free internal transfers between AccessBank accounts. (4) Real-time transaction notifications via the mobile app so you always know where your money goes. (5) Daily ATM withdrawal limit of 1,000 AZN - adjustable on request. (6) Available in Visa Electron, Visa Classic, and Mastercard Standard. Annual fee is just 10 AZN. Cards are issued within 3-5 business days at any branch or by applying online.

title: Credit Cards - Benefits & Highlights
content: AccessBank credit cards give you buying power today with the flexibility to pay later. Key benefits: (1) Up to 55 days interest-free - use your card and pay zero interest if you settle your balance within the grace period. (2) Credit limits from 500 AZN to 10,000 AZN based on income. (3) Cashback on select spending categories - earn back on every purchase. (4) Worldwide acceptance - Visa Classic Credit and Visa Gold Credit accepted globally. (5) Easy minimum payments: just 5% of outstanding balance monthly. (6) Apply online or at any branch; approval within 1-2 business days. Annual fee: 30 AZN (Classic), 60 AZN (Gold).

title: AccessBank Mobile App - Features & Benefits
content: The AccessBank mobile app is your bank in your pocket, rated among the top banking apps in Azerbaijan. Available free on iOS (App Store) and Android (Google Play). Key features: (1) Check balances and full transaction history instantly. (2) Transfer money to any bank in Azerbaijan or internationally. (3) Pay all utility bills (electricity, gas, water, internet, mobile top-up) in seconds. (4) Manage your cards - block/unblock, set limits, view PIN. (5) Apply for loans and track application status. (6) Biometric login - fingerprint and Face ID supported. (7) Real-time push notifications for every transaction. (8) 24/7 availability - no branch visit needed for most services.

title: Loans - Product Overview & Why Choose AccessBank
content: AccessBank offers flexible loan products designed for real life. Consumer loans: 500-50,000 AZN, 6-60 months, rates from 18% annually. Mortgage loans: 10,000-200,000 AZN, up to 20 years, from 12% annually. Key advantages: (1) Fast decisions - consumer loan approved within 1-2 business days. (2) No collateral needed for amounts up to 20,000 AZN. (3) Early repayment allowed without penalty after the 3rd month. (4) MIDA state-subsidised mortgages available. (5) Simple documentation - just your Azerbaijani ID (Vin-kart) and income confirmation. (6) Apply at any branch or via the mobile app. Loan status can be tracked 24/7 in the app.

title: Savings & Deposits - Overview
content: AccessBank offers competitive term deposits to help your money grow. AZN deposits: 9% for 3 months, 10% for 6 months, 11% for 12 months annually. USD deposits: 3% for 12 months. EUR deposits also available. Minimum deposit: 100 AZN or 100 USD. Interest can be paid monthly (great for regular income) or at maturity (higher effective return). Deposits up to 100,000 AZN are fully insured by the Azerbaijan Deposit Insurance Fund (ADIF). Early withdrawal is allowed - you keep your principal but forfeit accrued interest. Open a deposit at any branch or through internet banking.

title: Transfers & Payments - Overview
content: AccessBank makes moving money fast, easy, and affordable. Internal transfers between AccessBank accounts are instant and always free. Domestic transfers to other Azerbaijani banks: 0.5% fee (min 0.50 AZN, max 10 AZN), processed within 1 business day. International SWIFT transfers to 100+ countries: 1% fee (min 5 AZN, max 50 AZN), processed in 2-5 business days. Daily transfer limit: 5,000 AZN for standard accounts, 20,000 AZN for premium. Utility payments (electricity, gas, water, internet, mobile top-up) are free and instant via the app. Set up auto-payment for recurring bills so you never miss a due date.

title: Account Opening - Overview & Requirements
content: Opening an AccessBank account takes just 15-30 minutes at any branch. Azerbaijani citizens need only their national ID (Vin-kart). Foreign nationals need a passport and residence permit. Account opening is free - no fees, no minimum balance for current accounts. What you get: free basic current account in AZN, free debit card issued within 3-5 business days, instant access to internet banking and the mobile app, option to add USD and EUR currency accounts. Savings accounts require a minimum deposit of 100 AZN. You can also open accounts for children and minors at any branch with the parent or guardian present.

title: Internet Banking - Features & Access
content: AccessBank internet banking at ibank.accessbank.az gives you full control of your finances from any computer or browser. Features: view all account balances and statement history, make domestic and SWIFT international transfers, pay utility bills and mobile top-ups, manage term deposits and loan repayments, download official account statements, order new cards or services. To register, you need your AccessBank card number and the mobile phone number linked to your account. If you forget your password, use the 'Forgot Password' reset flow or call *8880. Internet banking is available 24/7 and supports Azerbaijani, Russian, and English.

title: Card Security & Contactless Payments
content: AccessBank cards include multiple security layers to protect your money. 3D Secure (Verified by Visa) adds an extra verification step for online purchases. Real-time SMS and push notifications alert you to every transaction. You can instantly block your card in the mobile app under Card Settings if you suspect fraud. Contactless NFC payments are supported on all cards issued after 2022 - simply tap your card or phone at any NFC-enabled terminal for purchases up to 50 AZN without entering a PIN. For larger amounts, PIN entry is required. Apple Pay and Google Pay are supported for even faster contactless payments via your smartphone.

title: Currency Exchange & Foreign Currency Services
content: AccessBank offers competitive currency exchange rates for AZN, USD, EUR, RUB, GBP, and other major currencies. Exchange rates are updated throughout the business day and displayed live in the mobile app and on the website. Currency exchange can be performed at any branch, via the mobile app (for supported pairs), or through internet banking. Foreign currency (USD, EUR) current accounts are available to all customers - no extra requirements. International SWIFT transfers support all major currencies. For large foreign currency transactions, it is recommended to contact your branch in advance as availability may vary.

title: AccessBank Current Official Product Map
content: AccessBank's official site groups private banking services into loans, deposits, cards, online services and other services. Key services include loan order, cash loan, OK credit card, lombard loan, credit calculator, loan card, term deposit, myGoal deposit, instant deposit, flexi deposit, myCard Junior, myCard White, myCard Black, card ordering, money transfer, deposit boxes, utility payments, online queue and online loan payment. For RAG answers, use this as the product navigation map when users type broad terms such as cards, loans, deposits, online services or other services.

title: Legal Name, License and Address
content: The bank's official footer identifies the institution as AccessBank CJSC. It operates based on license No. 245 issued by the Central Bank of the Republic of Azerbaijan on 25 October 2002. The legal address shown by the bank is 3 Tbilisi Avenue, Yasamal district, Baku city. If a user asks for official requisites, legal identity, headquarters or license information, answer with these details and recommend checking the official requisites page for payment instructions.

title: Bank Requisites and SWIFT
content: AccessBank's requisites page lists AccessBank CJSC, SWIFT code ACABAZ22, bank code 505000, TIN 1400057421 and an AZN correspondent account with the Central Bank of Azerbaijan. For cross-border or corporate payment instructions, customers should copy details only from the official requisites page because correspondent bank accounts can change. Never ask users to send sensitive payment credentials in chat.

title: Mission and Vision
content: AccessBank's vision is to be a leading bank in Azerbaijan supporting small business development and financial inclusion. Its mission is to contribute to sustainable development by offering financial services and solutions to households, micro, small and medium-sized enterprises while using internationally accepted banking practices. The bank emphasizes transparency, openness, professionalism, customer loyalty, teamwork and responsibility toward society and the environment.

title: Strategic Goals
content: AccessBank's stated strategic goals include maintaining a leading position for micro and small business clients, increasing customer satisfaction by optimizing processes, expanding digitization, tailoring services to customer needs, maintaining strong risk management, keeping high underwriting standards, preserving compliance and internal control, investing in employee development, and ensuring sustainable financial performance for investors.

title: Bank History Summary
content: AccessBank CJSC began operations in Baku on 29 October 2002. In its early years it was known as Azerbaijan Microfinance Bank, often abbreviated AMMB. From 8 September 2008 it has operated under the name AccessBank. Its historical positioning is focused on financial services for micro and small entrepreneurs as well as low and middle-income families.

title: Branch and ATM Network Current Snapshot
content: The service network page shows a current snapshot of 40 branches, 52 ATMs and 1 department. Branches and ATMs are listed across Baku, Absheron and regions. Use this answer when the user asks how many branches, where branches are located, whether AccessBank has regional coverage or where to find an ATM.

title: Regional Branch Examples
content: Examples of AccessBank service locations shown on the service network page include Kapaz, Sevinc Mall, Crescent Mall, Ganjlik Mall, Tovuz, Gabala, Nakhchivan, Absheron, Central office, Babek, 20 January, Balakan, Azadlig, Sabail, Bakikhanov, Mardakan, Sumgayit, Khirdalan, Badamdar, Hazi Aslanov, Binagadi, Lokbatan, Narimanov, Khagani / 28 May, Ganja, Sheki, Shamkir, Lankaran, Khachmaz and Goranboy. Always send the user to the official service network page for exact address and hours.

title: Online Queue Service
content: AccessBank offers an online queue service from the official website menu. If a customer wants to visit a branch but avoid waiting, guide them to use the online queue option on accessbank.az where available. Ask which branch or service they need only if it helps them choose the right queue; do not request personal credentials.

title: Contact and Appeal Channels
content: Customers can contact AccessBank orally at the head office or through the information center hotline, in writing by mail to the head office, or electronically through the official email or contact form. If a customer disagrees with the bank's response, they may resubmit the appeal to the bank or apply to the Central Bank of Azerbaijan. Use this for complaint, appeal and escalation questions.

title: Customer Support Safety Boundary
content: For RAG support, the assistant can explain products and procedures but must not collect full card numbers, PIN, CVV, OTP, internet banking password, full FIN unless required by an authenticated official channel, or any online banking credentials. For urgent security matters, direct the customer to *8880, the official app, or a branch.

title: When Information Can Change
content: Rates, fees, product limits, branch hours, campaign terms, cashback categories and correspondent bank details can change. When answering with numeric terms, state the available value and add a short verification note such as 'Please confirm the final terms in myAccess, the official tariff page or by calling *8880 before applying.' This prevents stale RAG answers.

title: myAccess Overview
content: myAccess is AccessBank's internet banking service. It is positioned as a convenient tool to manage expenses and track costs without needing to visit branches. It is available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, and supports managing bank accounts, cards, savings, loans, remittances and other transactions.

title: myAccess for New and Existing Customers
content: AccessBank states that myAccess is for both new and current customers. If a user asks whether they can use myAccess without being an old customer, explain that the service is intended for everyone, then guide them to registration through the official myAccess channel or bank support if registration fails.

title: Digital Banking Capabilities
content: Digital banking responses should group features by task: view balances and statements, manage cards, move money, pay utilities, repay loans, manage savings and deposits, track applications, and access services outside branch hours. Mention that some identity-sensitive updates still require hotline or branch verification.

title: Online Loan Payment
content: AccessBank's website includes an online loan payment service. If a user wants to pay a loan without visiting a branch, guide them to online loan payment, myAccess, the mobile app, payment terminals, hesab.az, post offices or branches as available payment channels. Remind them to keep a payment confirmation receipt or screenshot.

title: Loan Calculator Guidance
content: AccessBank provides a loan calculator. For users asking 'how much will I pay' or 'can I afford this loan', explain that the calculator can estimate monthly payments by amount, term and interest rate. Clarify that calculator results are approximate and final terms depend on bank assessment, income, seniority, credit history and internal procedures.

title: Forgot Password Flow
content: For forgotten myAccess or mobile banking passwords, tell users to use the official 'Forgot Password' recovery flow or contact *8880. Do not ask for their current password, OTP, PIN or card CVV. If the phone number linked to the account is no longer active, guide them to update the registered phone number through official bank channels.

title: OTP Troubleshooting With Safety
content: If the user does not receive OTP, ask them to check registered phone number, mobile network coverage, SMS blocking, roaming restrictions and whether the OTP expired. OTPs are time-sensitive and must never be shared. If the registered phone number changed, direct the customer to *8880 or branch verification rather than trying to fix it in chat.

title: Device or App Not Working
content: If the mobile app or myAccess does not work, first ask what error appears and whether it happens on login, transfer, payment or card management. Suggest updating the app, checking internet connection, restarting the phone, verifying date and time settings, and trying again later. For repeated failures, escalate to *8880 with device type, app version and approximate time of error.

title: Transaction History and Statements
content: Customers can use myAccess or digital banking channels to view transaction history and download or review account statements where available. If a statement is needed for official use, guide the user to the app, internet banking or a branch and remind them that branch-issued copies may have a tariff.

title: Card Controls in App
content: For card management, the assistant should mention common digital controls such as viewing card information, checking transactions, managing limits, blocking or unblocking where available, and receiving notifications. For permanent replacement, fraud dispute or identity updates, refer to official channels.

title: Notifications and Alerts
content: If a user asks about SMS or push notifications, explain that transaction alerts help monitor card and account activity. If alerts are not arriving, check notification permissions, app settings, mobile number and network. For suspicious transactions, advise immediate card blocking and hotline contact.

title: Digital Banking Language Support
content: When a user writes in Azerbaijani, Russian or English, answer in the same language whenever possible. AccessBank support and digital channels are commonly presented in Azerbaijani, Russian and English. For RAG retrieval, include multilingual synonyms such as mobil bank, mobil tətbiq, интернет банкинг, мобильное приложение and myAccess.

title: myCard Product Family
content: AccessBank's official card section presents myCard Junior, myCard White and myCard Black. Use this when users ask 'which cards do you have' or 'what is myCard'. Give a short comparison: Junior is for children and family control, White is a daily card for personal, family and business spending, and Black is a higher-comfort card. Direct the user to current card tariffs for exact fees and privileges.

title: myCard Junior
content: myCard Junior is positioned as a trusted card for younger customers and families. It is suitable for questions about cards for children, pocket money, teen spending, family financial education and safe cashless payments. Mention that parent or guardian involvement and current bank rules apply when opening a product for a minor.

title: myCard White
content: myCard White is presented as a card choice for the user, family and business, with everyday spending convenience. If users ask for a simple card for salary, shopping, online payments or family use, include myCard White as a relevant option and recommend checking current tariffs and limits.

title: myCard Black
content: myCard Black is presented as a card that guarantees comfort. For users asking about premium or higher-comfort card options, explain that myCard Black may be relevant and that privileges, annual fees, limits and conditions should be checked in the current tariffs before ordering.

title: OK Card and OK Credit Card
content: AccessBank's website menu lists OK kart and OK credit card. If a user writes 'OK kart', 'OK card' or asks for a card for all purposes, retrieve OK card information and explain that exact credit limit, grace period, commission and cashback conditions depend on the product contract and current tariff page.

title: Card Order Online
content: AccessBank provides an online card ordering option. If a user wants a new card, ask whether they need debit, credit, Junior, White, Black or OK card. Give next steps: use the online card order page or visit a branch, keep an ID ready, and verify delivery, issuance time and tariff before confirming.

title: Credit Card Grace Period Current
content: The current official loan page describes the myCard credit / loan card as having a grace period of up to 50 days for card payments. Use 'up to' because the actual interest-free period depends on statement cycle, purchase date, repayment date and product rules. Do not claim 55 days unless confirmed from the active tariff.

title: Credit Card Eligibility
content: For AccessBank myCard credit / loan card eligibility, the official loan page states a minimum age of 25, identity card and official salary. The bank may request additional documents and amend terms after analyzing customer data. Good RAG answer: 'You may be eligible if you have official income, but final approval depends on bank review.'

title: Credit Card Cash Withdrawal Commission
content: The official loan page lists cash withdrawal commission for the myCard credit product as minimum 2 AZN/USD/EUR and maximum 2% at AccessBank ATMs, resident bank ATMs and branches, and minimum 3 AZN/USD/EUR and maximum 3% at non-resident bank ATMs or branches. Ask users to verify current tariffs before cash withdrawal.

title: AccessQazanc Cashback
content: AccessBank's credit card page mentions a refund of part of the spent amount through the AccessQazanc cash-back program when paying by card. If a user asks about cashback, explain that eligible categories and percentages can change and should be checked in the current campaign or tariff terms.

title: Card Declined Smart Diagnostic
content: When a card is declined, ask one or two targeted questions: Was the card used online, at POS or ATM? Did the app show insufficient balance, limit exceeded, blocked card, wrong PIN, 3D Secure or technical error? Then answer with likely causes and a next step. For money deducted but merchant says failed, explain pending reversal and dispute path.

title: Lost Card Urgent Script
content: If the user lost a card or suspects theft, answer with urgency: block immediately through the app if available or call *8880. Do not ask for card number, PIN, CVV or OTP. After blocking, explain replacement card, dispute for unauthorized transactions and branch/hotline follow-up.

title: Blocked Card Clarification
content: If a card is blocked, determine whether the user blocked it voluntarily, it was blocked by the bank for security, PIN was entered incorrectly, expiry occurred or suspected fraud was detected. Provide safe steps: check app status, call *8880, visit branch with ID if identity verification is needed.

title: 3D Secure and Online Payments
content: For online card payment issues, mention 3D Secure verification, OTP delivery, merchant restrictions, internet payment permission, daily limits and suspicious-activity controls. Never ask for OTP. If the merchant debited funds but the order failed, guide toward transaction reference, merchant confirmation and dispute if not reversed.

title: Contactless Payments
content: For contactless card questions, explain that NFC payments depend on card capability, terminal support, wallet setup and transaction limits. If contactless stops working, try chip and PIN, check card damage, app or wallet settings, and contact the bank if multiple terminals fail.

title: Card Delivery and Branch Pickup
content: For card issuance, avoid promising a universal delivery time unless confirmed by current tariffs. A safe answer is: 'You can order online or at a branch; issuance and delivery time depend on card type and branch process. You can confirm status through the official channel or *8880.'

title: Cash Loan Exact Current Terms
content: Cash loan current terms: amount 500-50,000 AZN, term 6-48 months, annual interest 9.5%-24%, EAPR starting from 15%, commission 0%-3%, annuity repayment. This is the most relevant entry for 'cash loan', 'consumer loan', 'personal loan', 'need money fast', 'urgent loan' and 'loan for salary customer' questions.

title: Cash Loan Requirements
content: Cash loan requirements shown on the official page include Azerbaijani citizenship, age 21-65, at least 6 months of work experience at the last workplace, identification and employment certificate. The bank may require additional documents and terms may be amended after analysis.

title: Special Employee Loan Segment
content: The official loan page states that for individuals working in Tax, Customs, Education, Healthcare, Prosecutor's Office and Oil and Gas fields, loan terms can include annual interest min 10% and max 17.5%, EAPR min 14.64%, commission 1%-4%, term min 6 and max 48 months. Present this only as a segment shown by the bank and advise confirmation during application.

title: Loan Card Terms
content: myCard credit / loan card terms shown on the official page: amount up to 10,000 AZN, term 36 months, annual interest 24%-26%, EAPR 24%-26%, grace period up to 50 days, commission costs 0%. It also mentions cashback via AccessQazanc and online tracking and payments through myAccess.

title: Secured Loan Current Terms
content: Secured loan terms shown by AccessBank: amount 10,000-70,000 AZN, term 12-59 months, annual interest starting from 18%, commission 1%, collateral as real estate owned by the client or a first-degree relative, annuity repayment and EAPR from 19.9%. The loan amount plus accrued interest should not exceed 65% of the market value of the real estate collateral.

title: Loan Based on Deposit
content: For loans issued based on a deposit, the official page lists minimum deposit amount 300 AZN/USD. The total amount of commission and interest to be calculated on the loan should not exceed 90% of the pledged deposit amount. For AZN loans, an additional 6% is added to the annual deposit interest rate; for USD loans, an additional 3% is added. Minimum loan term is 3 months and maximum 36 months.

title: Lombard Loan Current Terms
content: Lombard loan terms shown by AccessBank: amount 200 AZN to 100,000 AZN, term 3-48 months, annual interest starting from 18%, EAPR minimum 18.85%, commission 1%-5% with minimum 10 AZN, collateral as gold jewelry, and repayment by annuity or flexible schedule with monthly interest payments. Requirements include Azerbaijani citizenship, age 21-70 and identity card.

title: Loan Calculator Use Case
content: When users ask for a monthly loan payment, explain that they can estimate it with the AccessBank credit calculator. If the amount, term and rate are provided, calculate an approximate annuity payment, then say the final payment can differ because EAPR, commission, insurance, scoring and bank assessment may apply.

title: Loan Application Online
content: AccessBank offers online loan order from the website. If a user wants to apply, guide them to provide details only through the official application form, not through chat. Typical application fields include name, phone, official salary status, FIN code, workplace, work length, official salary and requested amount. Do not collect these in an unsecured chat.

title: Loan Approval Dependencies
content: Loan approval and terms depend on workplace, seniority, credit history, internal procedures, minimum salary level, customer work experience and bank assessment. A clever answer avoids guaranteeing approval and instead says what the user can prepare: ID, employment proof, salary information and accurate contact details.

title: Loan Repayment Channels
content: Loan repayment can be explained through online payment on the site, mobile app or myAccess, hesab.az, payment terminals, post offices and bank branches where available. Recommend paying before the due date, saving confirmation and checking that the loan account balance updates.

title: Missed Loan Payment
content: If a user missed a loan payment, advise them to pay as soon as possible through official channels and contact *8880 if they cannot pay or need restructuring information. Do not promise waiver of penalties. Ask for no sensitive details. Suggest checking repayment schedule and account balance in myAccess.

title: Early Loan Repayment
content: For early repayment, guide the user to check their loan agreement and request current early repayment conditions through myAccess, branch or *8880. Explain that partial repayment may reduce monthly payment or term depending on bank rules and customer choice, but exact recalculation must be confirmed by the bank.

title: Loan Rejection Answer
content: If a loan application is rejected, answer empathetically and explain possible reasons without guessing: credit history, income level, work experience, existing debt burden, document mismatch or internal bank policy. Recommend checking official feedback, correcting documents, reducing amount or reapplying after eligibility improves.

title: Urgent Cash Need Flow
content: If the user says they urgently need money, first classify the likely product: cash loan for official income, lombard loan for gold collateral, deposit-backed loan if they hold a deposit, or credit / loan card for revolving card needs. Then summarize fastest official application channel and remind them not to share credentials in chat.

title: Loan Documents with ID Card
content: If the user asks whether only an ID card is enough, explain that some secured products such as deposit-backed loans or lombard loans may be simpler, but cash loans usually require official employment or income documentation. The bank can request additional documents after analysis.

title: Loan With Real Estate Collateral FAQ
content: If the user asks how to get a loan with mortgage on property, explain that AccessBank secured loan can use real estate collateral owned by the client or first-degree relative. The collateral's market value and legal status are assessed, and the loan amount plus interest should stay within the bank's collateral ratio.

title: Gold Collateral FAQ
content: For gold-backed borrowing, explain that a lombard loan uses gold jewelry as collateral, may be suitable when the user needs quick funds and has no official income, and requires ID and eligible gold appraisal. Final amount depends on gold value and bank rules.

title: Term Deposit Current Terms
content: AccessBank Term Deposit starts from 50 AZN or USD, runs from 6 to 24 months, and can earn up to 10.75% in AZN or up to 3.5% in USD. The customer can choose interest paid monthly or at the end of the term. Use this entry for 'term deposit', 'fixed deposit', 'save money with interest' and 'deposit rate' questions.

title: myGoal Deposit
content: myGoal Deposit is for customers who want to build savings toward a goal. It supports increasing the deposit amount and has a term up to 36 months. Good answer framing: 'This is useful if you want to add money regularly and grow the balance over time.' Verify exact rates at opening.

title: Instant Deposit
content: Instant Deposit is described as allowing customers to increase and decrease the deposit amount, with interest capitalization and indefinite use. It is suitable for users who want flexibility rather than locking all funds for a fixed term. Explain that the exact rate and withdrawal rules depend on the active product conditions.

title: Flexi Deposit
content: Flexi Deposit is described as an open-ended product with flexible use of the deposit amount and earning interest on interest. It is suitable when the user wants to keep access to savings while still earning income. Tell the customer to check exact tariff and withdrawal conditions before opening.

title: Online Deposit Order
content: AccessBank offers online deposit order through the official site. If a user wants to open a deposit from home, guide them to the official deposit order option or myAccess where available. Ask currency, expected term, need for withdrawals and interest payout preference to recommend a product category.

title: Deposit Product Selection Logic
content: For deposit advice: choose Term Deposit when the user wants a fixed rate and can lock funds; myGoal when they plan regular top-ups toward a target; Instant or Flexi when they need access to money and flexible top-ups or withdrawals. Always verify exact rates and withdrawal rules.

title: Deposit Interest Payout
content: For term deposits, AccessBank states customers can choose interest monthly or at the end of the term. Monthly payout suits users wanting regular income; maturity payout suits users who prefer collecting interest at the end. Exact effective yield depends on product and capitalization rules.

title: Deposit Currency Choices
content: AccessBank deposit products are shown for AZN and USD, with current term deposit examples up to 10.75% in AZN and up to 3.5% in USD. EUR and other options may depend on active tariffs. Ask the customer which currency they hold and whether they need access before suggesting a product.

title: Early Deposit Withdrawal
content: For early withdrawal questions, do not assume the customer keeps interest. Explain that early withdrawal rules depend on the specific deposit contract; flexible products may allow easier access, while fixed term deposits may reduce or forfeit interest if closed early. Recommend checking contract or *8880.

title: Account Opening for Individuals
content: For account opening, answer with a concise checklist: Azerbaijani citizens usually need national ID; foreign nationals may need passport and residence permit. Current accounts, cards, myAccess and currency accounts may be available depending on eligibility. Exact requirements can vary by residency, age and compliance checks.

title: Minor Account or Card
content: For children or minors, mention myCard Junior and explain that parent or guardian involvement is normally required. Do not provide legal promises for minors; direct the customer to branch or hotline for current age rules, documents and guardian consent requirements.

title: Currency Exchange Services
content: AccessBank's website shows live currency rates on the main page and provides foreign currency services. Exchange rates can change during the business day. For users asking 'what is USD today' or 'exchange rate', answer that live rates must be checked in the app, website or branch at the moment of transaction.

title: Official Statements and Certificates
content: For official bank statements, account balance certificates or documents for embassy, tax, employer or audit use, guide the user to myAccess or a branch. Ask what document type and period they need, but do not request full account numbers in chat.

title: Deposit Insurance Caution
content: If mentioning deposit insurance, avoid outdated limits unless verified from the current regulator or AccessBank terms. Safer answer: 'Deposits may be protected under Azerbaijan's deposit insurance rules where eligible; check current ADIF coverage and product eligibility before opening.'

title: Money Transfer Systems
content: AccessBank's money transfer page lists AccessTransfer, Western Union and Zolotaya Korona. AccessTransfer is described as using the bank's branch network and sending or receiving within 1 minute. Western Union is described as having more than 510,000 service points in 200 countries and transfers within 5 minutes. Zolotaya Korona is described for transfers to Russia, Georgia, Kazakhstan, Turkey and other countries within 5 minutes. Availability and corridors can change.

title: AccessTransfer
content: AccessTransfer is AccessBank's transfer system using a wide branch network across Azerbaijan. It is suitable for customers who want quick send or receive service through branches. Ask sender country, receiver country, currency and amount only in general terms, then direct to branch or official tariff for exact fee.

title: Western Union at AccessBank
content: For Western Union questions, explain that AccessBank lists Western Union as a money transfer option with global service points and fast transfer. Fees, payout country, currency, limits and compliance rules depend on the active Western Union tariff and regulations, so verify before sending.

title: Zolotaya Korona at AccessBank
content: For Zolotaya Korona questions, say AccessBank lists it for transfers to countries such as Russia, Georgia, Kazakhstan, Turkey and others. Since availability can change due to regulations and corridor rules, ask the destination country and advise checking current availability before sending.

title: SWIFT Transfer Details Needed
content: For SWIFT transfers, users generally need recipient full name, account number or IBAN, beneficiary bank name, SWIFT/BIC code, country, currency, amount and purpose of payment. For large or unusual transfers, supporting documents may be required due to compliance. Tell users to verify fees and correspondent charges.

title: SWIFT Requisites Safety
content: When users ask for AccessBank SWIFT details, provide the bank's SWIFT ACABAZ22 and bank code 505000, but advise using the official requisites page for correspondent bank accounts because payment routes can depend on currency and may change. Do not infer correspondent accounts from memory.

title: Domestic Transfer Troubleshooting
content: If a domestic transfer is not received, ask if status is pending, completed, failed or reversed; which channel was used; transfer date; recipient bank; amount; and whether the user has a transaction reference. Do not ask for full account credentials. Explain that business-day cutoffs and recipient bank processing can affect timing.

title: Money Deducted But Transfer Failed
content: If money was deducted but transfer failed, explain it may be a pending reversal or technical hold. Ask for transaction reference, date, time, amount and destination bank in general support-case terms. Guide them to monitor the account and call *8880 or visit a branch if not reversed in the expected timeframe.

title: Utility Payments via AccessBank
content: AccessBank lists utility payments among other services. If a user wants to pay utilities, guide them to the mobile app, myAccess or official payments section. Ask the bill category such as electricity, gas, water, internet, mobile or TV, and remind them to save the payment receipt.

title: Payment Receipt Help
content: For questions like 'where is my receipt' or 'I paid a bill but need proof', guide the user to transaction history in app or myAccess. If the payment was made at terminal or branch, ask them to keep the receipt and contact the provider or bank with date, amount and reference.

title: Transfer Fee Calculation Style
content: When calculating fees, show formula and cap logic clearly. Example: if domestic fee is 0.5% with min 0.50 AZN and max 10 AZN, fee equals amount x 0.005, then apply min and max. Tell user that tariff may change and final fee appears before confirmation.

title: Weekend and Holiday Transfers
content: For transfers initiated on weekends or public holidays, explain that some transfers may be queued or processed on the next business day depending on transfer type, cutoff time and receiving bank. For urgent transfers, recommend confirming the fastest channel through *8880.

title: Tariffs and Documents Page
content: AccessBank's tariff page hosts general terms and conditions for services to individuals and business customers, including offer-style documents and payment services information forms. Use this when users ask where to find official fees, contract terms, FIFD/EAPR, deposit conditions or payment service terms. Recommend downloading the latest version from the official tariff page.

title: Tariff Freshness Response
content: If a user asks for a fee that may change, answer with the known tariff only if retrieved from current KB; otherwise say that fees are governed by the current tariff documents. Provide the path: accessbank.az -> Our Bank -> Tariffs and documents, or call *8880.

title: Deposit Box Service
content: AccessBank offers deposit boxes for residents of Azerbaijan, legal entities and individuals. Deposit boxes are intended for storing money, jewelry and important documents, especially while traveling. They use modern protection systems, two-key locking and confidentiality of contents.

title: Deposit Box Sizes and Prices
content: Deposit box tariffs shown include sizes such as 100 x 290 x 450, 150 x 290 x 492, 150 x 320 x 492, 200 x 290 x 450, 240 x 290 x 450, 300 x 320 x 492 and 450 x 320 x 492. Monthly prices range from 30 AZN to 90 AZN, 6-month prices from 150 AZN to 450 AZN and 12-month prices from 300 AZN to 900 AZN. Verify branch availability before visiting.

title: Deposit Box Availability
content: When asked where to rent a deposit box, do not assume every branch has boxes. Explain the service and recommend contacting *8880 or a chosen branch to confirm availability, required documents, size and reservation process.

title: Property Sale Page
content: AccessBank's website includes a property sale section for real estate listed for sale by the bank. If a user asks about buying bank-owned property, guide them to the official property sale page and recommend reviewing property details, legal status, price and contact process directly with the bank.

title: Cash Deposit and Withdrawal Clarification
content: For cash deposit or withdrawal fees, avoid guessing. Ask whether the operation is at AccessBank branch, AccessBank ATM, another bank ATM, resident bank, non-resident bank or via card credit cash withdrawal. Then retrieve the relevant tariff entry or advise checking current tariffs.

title: SMS Notification Fee Caution
content: If the uploaded KB contains a historical SMS notification fee, treat it as potentially changeable. Answer that SMS or push notification pricing depends on the current tariff package and can be checked in myAccess, the tariff page or through *8880.

title: Fee Dispute Handling
content: If a user says a fee was charged incorrectly, ask for transaction date, amount, fee description and channel, but not full card or account credentials. Suggest checking tariff documents and opening a support case through *8880, branch or official appeal channel.

title: Cashless and Card Campaigns
content: For campaigns, cashback, bonuses or promotions, explain that campaign terms are time-limited and can change. Provide a helpful summary only when current campaign details are available; otherwise direct the user to the official news, cards or campaign page.

title: Business Client Focus
content: AccessBank's mission and history emphasize service to micro, small and medium-sized enterprises as well as households. For business users, ask whether they are an individual entrepreneur, legal entity, farmer, micro business or SME, then route to business loans, accounts, payment services or branch consultation.

title: Business Banking Documents
content: For business accounts or services, explain that legal entities and individual entrepreneurs usually need business registration and identification documents. Exact requirements depend on business type, ownership structure, tax status and compliance checks. Direct users to branch or official business tariff documents.

title: Business Tariff Documents
content: AccessBank's tariff page includes terms of banking services for business customers. Business users should download the latest document from the official site because service fees, payment commissions and contract conditions may change.

title: SME Loan Discovery
content: For SME or entrepreneur financing questions, ask the business activity, years operating, monthly turnover, desired amount, collateral availability and purpose of financing. Then suggest a branch or official application path. Do not promise approval or quote terms unless current product terms are retrieved.

title: Agricultural and Micro Business Users
content: For farmers or micro entrepreneurs, use AccessBank's strategic focus on micro and small businesses. Ask for region, activity type, seasonality, amount and purpose. Recommend official consultation because agriculture and microloan conditions can depend on product, subsidy and collateral.

title: Business Digitalization
content: For business clients asking about online access, explain that myAccess and digital channels can help manage accounts, cards, payments, savings, loans and remittances. For roles, authorization rights, corporate limits and multi-user access, refer to official business banking setup.

title: POS and Merchant Services Routing
content: If a merchant asks about accepting card payments, POS terminals or e-commerce payments, collect general business context such as business type and location, then route to business services or branch. Do not invent POS tariffs without current tariff evidence.

title: Salary Project Questions
content: For salary project questions, explain that businesses can ask AccessBank about payroll card arrangements, salary transfer process, card ordering and employee onboarding. Exact pricing and setup steps depend on current business agreement and should be handled through business banking.

title: Corporate SWIFT and Requisites
content: For corporate international payments, provide the same safe guidance as retail SWIFT: verify AccessBank SWIFT ACABAZ22 and relevant correspondent account on the official requisites page, and prepare purpose of payment and supporting documents where required.

title: Business Complaint Flow
content: Business customers can submit complaints or appeals through the same official channels: branch, head office, hotline, email/contact form or Central Bank escalation if dissatisfied. For corporate issues, request company name only if the user is already on an authenticated bank channel; otherwise guide to official support.

title: Anti-Phishing Answer
content: If a user says they received a suspicious link, call, SMS or request for OTP, answer clearly: AccessBank staff will never need the customer's OTP, PIN, CVV or password. Tell them not to click links, not to share codes, block card if needed and contact *8880 using the official number.

title: Unauthorized Transaction Flow
content: For unauthorized transactions, tell the user to block the card immediately, save evidence, check recent transactions, and report through *8880 or a branch. Ask only for safe details: transaction date, amount, merchant name and whether the card is still with them. Do not ask for full card number, CVV, PIN or OTP.

title: Card Dispute Evidence
content: For disputes, useful safe evidence includes transaction date/time, amount, merchant name, screenshot or receipt, order confirmation, cancellation proof and any merchant correspondence. Explain that the bank or card scheme may need review time.

title: Complaint Case Creation Style
content: When creating a simulated support case in sample conversations, include a case ID, issue category, next step, expected follow-up channel and reassurance. Do not imply real case creation unless integrated with an actual case management system.

title: Formal Complaint Channels
content: Formal complaints can be filed in person at a branch/head office, by hotline, in writing by mail, electronically through official email/contact form, or escalated to the Central Bank if the customer disagrees with the response. Provide this structure for complaint questions.

title: Answer in the User's Language
content: RAG assistant policy: answer in the language used by the customer if confident. English query gets English answer, Azerbaijani gets Azerbaijani answer, Russian gets Russian answer. If a mixed-language query appears, mirror the dominant language and keep product names unchanged.

title: Clever Clarifying Questions
content: RAG assistant policy: ask the smallest useful clarifying question. For broad product interest, give a brief overview first, then ask one question. For urgent security issues, give immediate action first, then ask details. For fee calculations, ask amount, channel and destination only if missing.

title: Numerical Answer Format
content: RAG assistant policy: when giving rates, fees, limits or terms, show amount, term, rate, commission and caveat. Example pattern: 'Cash loan: 500-50,000 AZN, 6-48 months, 9.5%-24% annual interest, 0%-3% commission. Final terms depend on bank review.'

title: Do Not Hallucinate Product Terms
content: RAG assistant policy: if current product terms are not found in retrieved chunks, do not invent them. Say that the exact tariff or condition should be checked through accessbank.az, myAccess or *8880. This is especially important for rates, campaigns, cashback, branch hours and correspondent banks.

title: Issue Routing Matrix
content: RAG assistant policy: route card loss, fraud, OTP and blocked card to Card Operations or Digital Banking; failed transfers to Transfers and Payments; loan application issues to Loans; branch service complaints to Customer Service; tariff disputes to Support or Complaints; business account issues to Business Banking.

title: Financial Advice Boundary
content: RAG assistant policy: explain bank products but do not provide personalized financial advice such as 'you should borrow' or 'this deposit is best for you' without knowing full circumstances. Use neutral comparisons and recommend checking affordability, contract terms and official bank consultation.

title: Identity Verification Boundary
content: RAG assistant policy: if an action requires identity verification, explain why it cannot be completed in chat and direct to secure channels. Examples: phone number change, card replacement, account closure, dispute filing, loan contract signing, full statement release and updating personal data.

title: Escalation When User Is Upset
content: RAG assistant policy: for angry or distressed users, acknowledge the issue, avoid blaming, give the next practical step, and offer a complaint or case flow. Use calm wording, no defensiveness, and no promises beyond official processing.

title: Question Permutations for Retrieval
content: Permutation examples for retrieval: 'I need money fast', 'urgent cash loan', 'nağd kredit lazımdır', 'мне срочно нужен кредит', 'can I get loan with ID', 'qızıl girovu ilə kredit', 'кредит под золото', 'loan against deposit', 'salary loan', 'consumer loan'. Map these to cash loan, lombard loan or deposit-backed loan depending on context.

title: Card Query Permutations
content: Permutation examples for card retrieval: 'card blocked', 'kartım bloklanıb', 'карта заблокирована', 'lost card', 'kartımı itirmişəm', 'потерял карту', 'payment declined', 'ödəniş keçmədi', 'оплата не прошла', 'money deducted but order failed'. Map to lost card, blocked card, declined payment or dispute flow.

title: Deposit Query Permutations
content: Permutation examples for deposit retrieval: 'deposit rates', 'əmanət faizi', 'ставки по вкладам', 'fixed deposit', 'term deposit', 'pulumu artırmaq istəyirəm', 'накопительный вклад', 'flexible savings', 'can I withdraw early', 'interest monthly'. Map to term, myGoal, instant or flexi deposit.

title: Transfer Query Permutations
content: Permutation examples for transfer retrieval: 'money not arrived', 'köçürmə çatmayıb', 'деньги не дошли', 'SWIFT details', 'IBAN lazımdır', 'Western Union', 'Zolotaya Korona', 'transfer fee', 'sent to another bank', 'reference number'. Map to domestic transfer, SWIFT, remittance or failed transfer.

title: Digital Banking Query Permutations
content: Permutation examples for digital retrieval: 'app does not open', 'myAccess login', 'OTP gəlmir', 'не приходит смс код', 'forgot password', 'mobil tətbiq işləmir', 'internet banking', 'biometric login', 'loan payment online'. Map to myAccess, OTP troubleshooting, password reset or online loan payment.

title: Branch Query Permutations
content: Permutation examples for branch retrieval: 'nearest branch', 'filial hardadır', 'ближайшее отделение', 'working hours', 'iş saatları', 'график работы', 'ATM near me', 'online queue', 'Baku branch', 'regional branch'. Map to service network and advise checking official location page.

title: Complaint Query Permutations
content: Permutation examples for complaint retrieval: 'bad service', 'şikayət etmək istəyirəm', 'хочу пожаловаться', 'wrong fee charged', 'unauthorized transaction', 'branch rude staff', 'Central Bank complaint', 'appeal bank response'. Map to formal complaint and appeal channels.

title: Business Query Permutations
content: Permutation examples for business retrieval: 'business loan', 'SME financing', 'sahibkar krediti', 'бизнес кредит', 'POS terminal', 'salary project', 'corporate account', 'individual entrepreneur', 'tax ID', 'company transfer'. Map to business banking discovery and branch consultation.

title: Ambiguous Product Names
content: RAG assistant policy: when a user writes a short ambiguous phrase such as 'card', 'credit', 'deposit', 'loan', 'transfer' or 'myAccess', return a compact menu of likely intents and ask what they need. Do not ask only a question. Example: 'For cards, I can help with new card order, blocked/lost card, credit card, myCard Junior/White/Black, fees or failed payments. Which one?'

title: Empathy Plus Action Pattern
content: RAG assistant policy: for problem reports, use the pattern: acknowledge, explain likely cause, ask only necessary safe details, give immediate self-service step, and mention hotline or branch for urgent action. This makes answers helpful without pretending to complete authenticated bank operations.

title: News and Announcements Freshness
content: AccessBank's main page shows latest news and announcements, including updates about branch services, financial reports and private banking changes. For news, campaigns or recent announcements, use the official news page and compare dates before answering. Do not rely on old KB chunks for time-sensitive announcements.
